title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is a shift from ad hoc testing toward more formal, realistic, and deployment-relevant evaluation. New work spans guardrail guarantees, long-horizon agent benchmarks, confidence and trust calibration, and security controls for coding and cyber systems, while independent review and survey data add evidence on how frontier AI is actually being used and assessed.

items.title: Beyond Red-Teaming: Formal Guarantees of LLM Guardrail Classifiers
items.why_it_matters: Pushes safety filtering beyond empirical tests toward provable robustness claims for harmful-output classifiers.

items.title: WildClawBench: A Benchmark for Real-World, Long-Horizon Agent Evaluation
items.why_it_matters: Targets a major evaluation gap by testing agents on realistic, extended tasks instead of short synthetic benchmarks.

items.title: Engineering Robustness into Personal Agents with the AI Workflow Store
items.why_it_matters: Argues agent reliability needs software-engineering discipline, adversarial testing, and reusable workflows before deployment.

items.title: Evaluating the False Trust engendered by LLM Explanations
items.why_it_matters: Examines whether explanations increase user overtrust even when model answers remain unreliable.

items.title: Grounded or Guessing? LVLM Confidence Estimation via Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking
items.why_it_matters: Addresses a key multimodal safety issue: detecting confident answers that are not actually grounded in images.

items.title: Power Reinforcement Post-Training of Text-to-Image Models with Super-Linear Advantage Shaping
items.why_it_matters: Focuses on reward hacking in post-training, a core alignment risk for generative model optimization.

items.title: Running Codex safely at OpenAI
items.why_it_matters: Details concrete deployment safeguards for coding agents, including sandboxing, approvals, and network restrictions.

items.title: Review of the 'Risks from automated R&D' section in the Anthropic Risk Report (February 2026)
items.why_it_matters: Provides independent scrutiny of frontier-model risk claims around automated research and development capabilities.

items.title: Measuring the Self-Reported Impact of Early-2026 AI on Technical Worker Productivity
items.why_it_matters: Offers empirical evidence on real-world AI use, informing governance debates about capability uptake and deployment effects.

items.title: Scaling Trusted Access for Cyber with GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5-Cyber
items.why_it_matters: Shows a controlled-access governance model for high-risk cyber capabilities rather than open general release.

items.title: Shields to Guarantee Probabilistic Safety in MDPs
items.why_it_matters: Advances formal methods for keeping autonomous agents within quantified safety bounds under uncertainty.

items.title: Counterfactual Stress Testing for Image Classification Models
items.why_it_matters: Improves robustness evaluation for high-stakes models facing distribution shift and hidden failure modes.

items.title: MDrive: Benchmarking Closed-Loop Cooperative Driving for End-to-End Multi-agent Systems
items.why_it_matters: Moves autonomous-driving evaluation toward closed-loop, multi-agent safety testing instead of weaker open-loop metrics.

items.title: Is Your Driving World Model an All-Around Player?
items.why_it_matters: Highlights that realistic generation alone can mask failures in physics, planning, and safety-relevant driving behavior.

items.title: BEACON: A Multimodal Dataset for Learning Behavioral Fingerprints from Gameplay Data
items.why_it_matters: Contributes to continuous authentication research relevant to identity assurance in high-stakes digital systems.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
